page: Microsoft Azure D16als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 16 dedicated vCPUs, which map to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities. The processor achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench text processing. Single-core Geekbench scores are strong, and small-model LLM text generation is also strong. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present certain tradeoffs. While cached small-block read bandwidth is average, the uncached large-block read bandwidth is weak, scoring in the bottom 25%. Multi-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, fall within the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16als_v6 offers a streamlined hardware profile by reducing the memory-to-vCPU ratio to 1:2, making it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. This resource density minimizes overhead costs associated with unutilized RAM. Given these characteristics, the instance is well-suited for tasks such as web serving, text processing, software compilation, and lightweight single-threaded or moderately threaded applications where high clock speed is prioritized over large memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D16als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, representing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and bundled GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU execution and text processing, alongside strong single-core Geekbench results. However, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This low-memory configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand large memory footprints. It is best suited for web serving, text processing, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16als_v6 is a Dalv6-family VM featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, optimized for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for web and text processing.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a streamlined memory profile for high-frequency single-core workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Streamlined memory configuration of 32.0 GB RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage or bundled GPUs, but premium storage capable
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing
